seed: certainty vs curiosity; order_vs_chaos; rising_signal; bottom_to_top; tide against channels; receding_edges; straight_vs_curved; cage; relief_dread | abstract variation request 112; target batch count 200
title: Tides of Emotion
reasoning: This composition captures the ongoing struggle between certainty and curiosity through a split horizontal layout. The top half, embodying certainty, is composed of solid earthy tones like moss green and bone white, creating a sense of stability and order. These colors mirror the clarity and defined paths of structured thought. The bottom half, representing curiosity, introduces rust and stone gray in an undulating tide pattern, signifying dynamic exploration and uncharted territories. The collision occurs centrally, where the sharp, straight lines of the upper section begin to erode into the more fluid and chaotic forms below. The palette emphasizes resilience and fragility, balancing calmness with tension as colors interact and disrupt. Broken pixels sporadically punctuate both sections to signify imperfections in the emotional landscape, echoing the concept of evolving ideas amidst a backdrop of static.

layout.name: split_horizontal
layout.description: Top 4 rows are emotion A, bottom 4 rows are emotion B.

composition.name: inside_vs_outside
composition.idea: A small inner shape is surrounded by pressure, noise, or protection.

referenceExample.title: Pride Falling Into Shame
referenceExample.pattern: crown shape dissolving into dark stair steps

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
